# services.mattermost

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.mattermost`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.mattermost.configDir` | `absolute path` | Mattermost config directory. |
| `services.mattermost.dataDir` | `absolute path` | Mattermost working directory. |
| `services.mattermost.database.create` | `boolean` | Create a local PostgreSQL database for Mattermost automatically. |
| `services.mattermost.database.extraConnectionOptions` | `attribute set of (signed integer or string)` | Extra options that are placed in the connection URI's query parameters. |
| `services.mattermost.database.fromEnvironment` | `boolean` | Use services.mattermost.environmentFile to configure the database instead of writing the database URI to the Nix store. Useful if you use password authentication with peerAuth set to false. |
| `services.mattermost.database.host` | `string` | Host to use for the database. Can also be set to a path if you'd like to connect to a socket using a username and password. |
| `services.mattermost.database.name` | `string` | Local Mattermost database name. |
| `services.mattermost.database.password` | `string` | Password for local Mattermost database user. If set and peerAuth is not true, will cause a warning nagging you to use environmentFile instead since it will end up in the Nix store. |
| `services.mattermost.database.peerAuth` | `boolean` | If set, will use peer auth instead of connecting to a Postgres server. Use services.mattermost.database.socketPath to configure the socket path. |
| `services.mattermost.database.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port to use for the database. |
| `services.mattermost.database.socketPath` | `absolute path` | The database socket path. |
| `services.mattermost.database.user` | `string` | Local Mattermost database username. |
| `services.mattermost.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Mattermost chat server. |
| `services.mattermost.environment` | `attribute set of (signed integer or string)` | Extra environment variables to export to the Mattermost process from the systemd unit configuration. |
| `services.mattermost.environmentFile` | `null or absolute path` | Environment file (see {manpage}`systemd.exec(5)` "EnvironmentFile=" section for the syntax) which sets config options for mattermost (see [the Mattermost documentation](https://docs.mattermost.com/configure/configuration-settings.html#environment-variables)). Settings defined in the environment file will overwrite settings set via Nix or via the {option}`services.mattermost.extraConfig` option. Useful for setting config options without their value ending up in the (world-readable) Nix store, e.g. for a database password. |
| `services.mattermost.group` | `string` | Group which runs the Mattermost service. |
| `services.mattermost.host` | `string` | Host or address that this Mattermost instance listens on. |
| `services.mattermost.logDir` | `absolute path` | Mattermost log directory. |
| `services.mattermost.matterircd.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Mattermost IRC bridge. |
| `services.mattermost.matterircd.package` | `package` | The matterircd package to use. |
| `services.mattermost.matterircd.parameters` | `list of string` | Set commandline parameters to pass to matterircd. See <https://github.com/42wim/matterircd#usage> for more information. |
| `services.mattermost.mutableConfig` | `boolean` | Whether the Mattermost config.json is writeable by Mattermost. Most of the settings can be edited in the system console of Mattermost if this option is enabled. A template config using the options specified in services.mattermost will be generated but won't be overwritten on changes or rebuilds. If this option is disabled, persistent changes in the system console won't be possible (the default). If a config.json is present, it will be overwritten at service start! |
| `services.mattermost.package` | `package` | The mattermost package to use. |
| `services.mattermost.plugins` | `list of (absolute path or package)` | Plugins to add to the configuration. Overrides any installed if non-null. This is a list of paths to .tar.gz files or derivations evaluating to .tar.gz files. You can use `mattermost.buildPlugin` to build plugins; see the NixOS documentation for more details. |
| `services.mattermost.pluginsBundle` | `null or package` | Derivation building to a directory of plugin tarballs. This overrides {option}`services.mattermost.plugins` if provided. |
| `services.mattermost.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port for Mattermost server to listen on. |
| `services.mattermost.preferNixConfig` | `boolean` | If both mutableConfig and this option are set, the Nix configuration will take precedence over any settings configured in the server console. |
| `services.mattermost.settings` | `JSON value` | Additional configuration options as Nix attribute set in config.json schema. |
| `services.mattermost.siteName` | `string` | Name of this Mattermost site. |
| `services.mattermost.siteUrl` | `string` | URL this Mattermost instance is reachable under, without trailing slash. |
| `services.mattermost.socket.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Mattermost control socket. |
| `services.mattermost.socket.export` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Export socket control to system environment variables. |
| `services.mattermost.socket.path` | `absolute path` | Default location for the Mattermost control socket used by `mmctl`. |
| `services.mattermost.telemetry.enableDiagnostics` | `boolean` | True if we should enable sending diagnostic data. This reaches out to Mattermost's servers: https://docs.mattermost.com/manage/telemetry.html#error-and-diagnostics-reporting-feature |
| `services.mattermost.telemetry.enableSecurityAlerts` | `boolean` | True if we should enable security update checking. This reaches out to Mattermost's servers: https://docs.mattermost.com/manage/telemetry.html#security-update-check-feature |
| `services.mattermost.user` | `string` | User which runs the Mattermost service. |
